# UC-16.3.21 · Jira Epic Without Open Child Issues for Extended Period

## Description

Orphan epics mask portfolio reporting and waste capacity forecasts.

## Value

Keeps SAFe/PMO views honest by flagging empty epics.

## Implementation

Populate `child_open_count` via scheduled search into KV store lookup.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure: Nightly Jira REST export augmented with child counts in HEC.
• Data: Jira issues denormalized with `child_open_count` from ETL or lookup.
• For directory layout and inputs, see docs/implementation-guide.md.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Populate `child_open_count` via scheduled search into KV store lookup.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following search (then save as report or alert; tune time range and threshold):

```spl
index=itsm sourcetype="jira:cloud:issues" OR sourcetype="jira:issue"
| where issue_type="Epic" AND epic_status IN ("To Do","In Progress")
| eval stale_d=(now()-updated_epoch)/86400
| where stale_d > 14 AND child_open_count=0
| table key, epic_name, project, stale_d
| sort -stale_d
```

Understanding this search
Orphan epics mask portfolio reporting and waste capacity forecasts.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the Jira issue page, board, or service project view for the same dates. Confirm issue keys, status, and assignment fields; reconcile timestamp fields you use in the report.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add to a dashboard, wire alert actions, and document ownership. Suggested view: Table (epic list), Pie chart (by portfolio).

## SPL

```spl
index=itsm sourcetype="jira:cloud:issues" OR sourcetype="jira:issue"
| where issue_type="Epic" AND epic_status IN ("To Do","In Progress")
| eval stale_d=(now()-updated_epoch)/86400
| where stale_d > 14 AND child_open_count=0
| table key, epic_name, project, stale_d
| sort -stale_d
```

## Visualization

Table (epic list), Pie chart (by portfolio).
